sentences_input.text: [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] There were the major three synagogues or the central synagogue, which was a very large and quite beautiful edifice, built probably in the late '20s, maybe even the early '30s-- it must have been the late '20s-- [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] which [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] was, really, the representative institution of the community.

Right next to it, was a-- another, smaller known as the [NON-ENGLISH], which was the the home and center and prayer house of what is normally called [NON-ENGLISH], the less observant, less learned, less knowledgeable, but still quite Orthodox elements of the community.
The central synagogue was supposedly the home of the more advanced, more enlightened, less Orthodox element in the community.

And then there was a major Hasidic synagogue known as the [NON-ENGLISH], which was a kind of a catchall [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] institution, where [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] frequently conflicting Hasidic groups congregated.

[PAUSES FOR 5 SECONDS] Frequently, in, in the [NON-ENGLISH] and around the [NON-ENGLISH], there were quite virulent debates and even, I would say, physical altercations because of the conflicts between the various courts of various rabbis.

And then there were a very large number of smaller synagogues, usually attached to the home of one of the Hasidic rabbis in town, who may have been a descendant of very well-known wonder rabbis, but of lesser statue than their predecessors, but who have had their own adherents who congregated in those synagogues.
